text:
Market today: Chip stocks are the dominant story - a $2T rally in Q2 for Micron, Intel, and AMD confirms the AI/semiconductor theme is very much alive. Your book is built for exactly this environment, and momentum is with you for now.

Your holdings: All positions are on HOLD with gains - nothing is flashing red today. SOXL (+4.8%) is a 3x leveraged chip ETF that decays over time and can crater fast on any reversal - margin makes that doubly painful. SPAL is brand-new and very volatile; same decay risk applies. Both need tight attention. CRWD (+6.07%) is your strongest individual name - watch it doesn't get complacent without a tighter stop plan.

Watch: (1) Whether the chip rally broadens or starts showing rotation out after Q2's massive run - a $2T move invites profit-taking. (2) Bitcoin ETF volatility from headline #10 could spill sentiment into speculative tech names.

Bottom line: You're sitting on a solid green book in a hot sector - protect gains on your leveraged names and don't chase.
plays: SOXL: Consider tightening your stop above $200 default - after a 3x ETF gains ~5% on margin, a snap reversal hits hard; $230-235 zone is worth considering as a tighter floor.
SPAL: Brand-new leveraged ETF, fast mover, decay risk - keep position small and respect the $21.38 stop strictly; no reason to add here.

No new buys today - scanner shows ALAB as the cleanest setup (9/12, entry 482.54-481.96, stop 479.73) but it's in premium/chasing territory with no fresh catalyst from today's news to justify adding chip exposure when you're already heavily concentrated.
